_doc.pwm_min: Minimum PWM signal sent to motors in microseconds. Must match Q_M_PWM_MIN in params.parm. Typically 1000 µs (zero throttle).
_doc.pwm_max: Maximum PWM signal sent to motors in microseconds. Must match Q_M_PWM_MAX in params.parm. Typically 2000 µs (full throttle).
_doc.k_thrust: Lift motor thrust coefficient in Newtons. Each motor produces: T = k_thrust * throttle^2, where throttle = (PWM - pwm_min) / (pwm_max - pwm_min). Formula: k_thrust = (mass_kg * 9.81 / 4) / hover_throttle^2, where hover_throttle = Q_M_THST_HOVER from params.parm. Example: 10 kg, Q_M_THST_HOVER=0.35 → k_thrust = 24.525 / 0.1225 = 200.2 N.
_doc.k_torque: Lift motor reaction torque coefficient. Each motor also produces a spin torque: Q = k_torque * throttle^2. CW and CCW motors cancel in pairs, the net yaw torque comes from the imbalance. Typical ratio k_torque/k_thrust ≈ 0.01–0.05 m (effective moment arm of drag). Default 0.6 is an approximation — tune if yaw authority feels wrong.
_doc.k_thrust_pusher: Pusher (or puller) motor thrust coefficient in Newtons: T = k_thrust_pusher * throttle^2. Acts along body +X (forward). Leave at 0 if you have no fixed-wing motor. Pending real test-stand data for the T-Motor AT5220.
_doc.motor_time_constant_s: First-order lag time constant for motor thrust response, in seconds. Real brushless motors take 30–50 ms to spin up or down. Without this lag the simulator reacts to ArduPilot integrator steps as instant impulses, causing flip at liftoff. Typical value: 0.03–0.08 s.
_doc.rotational_damping: Rotational damping coefficient in N·m/(rad/s), applied equally to all three axes. Represents the combined effect of rotor-disk drag asymmetry and body aero drag that damps angular rate. Real multirotors have significant natural damping that the ArduPilot PIDs rely on — without this the hover PIDs go unstable. Tune: increase if the vehicle oscillates, decrease if response feels sluggish.
_doc.drag_coeff: Linear body drag coefficient in N/(m/s). Applied to translational velocity to produce a drag force opposing motion. Approximates fuselage and motor arm drag at low speed (below min_airspeed_ms where full aero is suppressed). Typical value: 0.1–0.5 N/(m/s).

_k_thrust_doc: 2026-07-02: was 200.2 — computed with the NAIVE formula (m·g/4)/THST_HOVER², which ignores AP's expo curve + spin window and gave TWR 7.4:1 (the exact sitl_ue takeoff-flip disease, BUGFIXES 2026-06-09). Derived through AP's full output path per ARCHITECTURE.md with the REAL 22-may Q_M_THST_HOVER 0.3488028: a=0.5112 (expo 0.65) -> u=0.15+0.8a=0.5590 (hover PWM 1559) -> kT=(10*9.81/4)/0.5590²=78.49 N. Max thrust/motor at SPIN_MAX 0.95: 70.8 N vs T-Motor P60 datasheet 6762 g = 66.3 N — consistent within 7%. TWR at full demand 2.89:1 (target 2-3).
_k_torque_doc: 2026-07-02: was 0.6 (ratio 0.6/200.2 = 0.003 m — far below the 0.01-0.05 m physical band; weak yaw). Physical estimate for Falcon CF 22x6.6: Q/T = (CQ/CT)·D ≈ 0.02-0.03 m -> k_torque = 78.49 × 0.025 ≈ 2.0. Replace with test-stand torque data when available.
_k_thrust_pusher_doc: 2026-07-02: set from the REAL datasheet value in VTOL_Electrical_Data.txt — AT5220-A max thrust 7516 g = 73.7 N at full throttle (u=1.0). Cruise check at the real AIRSPEED_CRUISE 24 m/s: parasite 7.2 + induced 3.1 + body 4.8 = 15.1 N -> u=0.45, matching the real TRIM_THROTTLE 45. AIRSPEED_MAX 28 needs 17.7 N -> u=0.49, ample margin. (Static-thrust coefficient; no prop-unloading model.)
_drag_coeff_doc: Hover heave (rotor-inflow) damping, BODY Z ONLY, faded out over hover_drag_fade band (see sitl_ue + INVARIANTS). Physical value rho*A_disk*v_i for 4x 22x6.6-inch props at 24.5 N hover thrust each: v_i=6.4 m/s -> 1.9 N·s/m per rotor -> 8. Descent check: 1.5 m/s descent sheds only 12 N of the 98 N hover thrust — no Q_M_SPIN_MIN floor risk (the sitl_ue PM3 descent-wobble mode).
_drag_coeff_hover_xy_doc: Mild hover translational damping (tau = m/c = 5 s) — gokce is the aerodynamically-faithful vehicle, so no stability-first inflation. Pusher punches through easily (65 N vs 12 N at the 6 m/s fade start).
_drag_coeff_forward_doc: Always-on isotropic parasite residual (the original drag_coeff value); wing CD0 covers the rest above stall.
